rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting component boundaries and valid scopes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Preserve the original AADL structure and do not introduce new components or connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
